title: Claude on AWS - Deployment Guide
description: Interactive guide for deploying Claude across different AWS offerings

content.heading: Subscribing to Claude for Enterprise on AWS Marketplace
content.description: Choose your subscription path to get started with Claude Enterprise on AWS. All paths lead to the same product — the difference is pricing and procurement process.

options.title: Self-Serve via AWS Marketplace
items: Minimum **20 seats** required
Single seat type: Enterprise seat (includes Claude web, desktop, mobile + Claude Code)
Billed annually, per user per month
Add seats anytime — prorated for remainder of billing cycle, charged immediately
Seat reductions only take effect at annual renewal

options.title: Adding Incremental Seats
items: Go to **Organization settings → Billing**
Click the pencil icon under **Seats**
Enter the number of additional seats needed
Review the prorated charge and confirm
Alternatively: a seat is auto-purchased when inviting a new member if none are available

options.title: Private Offer
items: Work with your Anthropic account team to request a private offer
Private offer appears in **AWS Marketplace → Manage subscriptions**
Review terms, pricing, and seat count in the offer details
Accept the offer in AWS Marketplace to activate your subscription

callout.text: 💡 **Note:** Once subscribed through any approach, the Primary Owner receives a setup email from Anthropic to create the workspace. Proceed to the "Setup Claude for Enterprise (with Entra ID)" tab for next steps.

subtabs.label: Setup Claude for Enterprise (with Entra ID)

content.heading: Setup Claude for Enterprise (with Entra ID)
content.description: Follow these steps sequentially to configure your Claude Enterprise workspace.

steps.title: Pre-requisites
steps.description: This guide assumes **Microsoft Entra ID** as your identity provider (IdP). Ensure the following teams and decisions are in place before proceeding.
items: **Infra / Cloud team** — will own the AWS Marketplace subscription and serve as the Primary Owner of the Claude workspace
**Identity team** — manages Microsoft Entra ID; will configure SSO (SAML 2.0) and optionally SCIM provisioning
**Domain verification** — add a DNS TXT record for your company domain (required before SSO can be enabled)
**Enterprise Application** — create a new Enterprise App in Entra ID for Claude (SAML 2.0)
**SCIM provisioning (optional)** — requires Entra ID P1 or P2 license; enables automatic user sync
**User/Group assignment** — identify which Entra groups or users will be assigned access to Claude

callouts.text: ⚠️ **Identify your Primary Owner:** There can be only one Primary Owner per workspace. This person is the super-user with full billing and admin control. You can have multiple Admins, but only one Primary Owner. Choose carefully — this should be someone from your Infra/Cloud team.

subsections.title: 🔐 Identity Team Preparedness
subsections.description: Brief your Identity team on what they'll need to do. They can start prepping in parallel while the Infra team handles subscription + Primary Owner setup:
items: **Domain verification** — add a DNS TXT record for your company domain (required before SSO can be enabled)
**Enterprise Application** — create a new Enterprise App in Entra ID for Claude (SAML 2.0)
**SCIM provisioning (optional)** — requires Entra ID P1 or P2 license; enables automatic user sync
**User/Group assignment** — identify which Entra groups or users will be assigned access to Claude

steps.title: Step 1: Primary Owner Setup
steps.description: The admin contact from the deal receives a setup email from Anthropic to create the workspace.
items: Go to **[https://claude.ai/login](https://claude.ai/login)**
Create your **workspace name** — this is your org's Claude environment
The Primary Owner account uses magic-link (passwordless email) login until SSO is configured

steps.title: Step 2: Configure SSO (Single Sign-On)
steps.description: Set up SAML 2.0 with Microsoft Entra ID. Expand each sub-step below — note the **owner** badge indicating who performs each action.
items: Go to [Claude Admin → Identity and access](https://claude.ai/admin-settings/identity)
Add your company domain and follow the verification steps
Requires adding a DNS TXT record (coordinate with your DNS admin)
In Identity and access settings, start the SSO configuration
Note the **Entity ID** and **Reply URL (ACS URL)** — share these with your Identity team
Keep this flow open while the Identity team configures Entra
In Entra Admin Center → Enterprise applications → New application
Search for "Claude" in gallery, or create your own app named "Claude"
Select "Integrate any other application you don’t find in the gallery"
Go to Single sign-on → SAML
In Basic SAML Configuration, enter the Entity ID and Reply URL from step 2b
Set Sign-on URL: `https://claude.ai/login`
In Attributes & Claims, set email claim to `user.mail`
Download the **Federation Metadata XML**
Identity team shares the Federation Metadata XML with Primary Owner
Primary Owner uploads it in the Claude SSO setup flow
Complete the connection wizard in Claude
Requires Entra ID P1 or P2 license
In the app → Provisioning → Set mode to Automatic
Enter Tenant URL and Secret Token from Claude setup flow
Ensure SCIM email attribute matches SSO email claim (`user.mail`)
Set Provisioning Status to On
In Users and groups, assign people or groups who should access Claude
Trigger a provisioning cycle (if SCIM enabled) and confirm users appear in Claude
Have a test user complete SSO login → verify they land in the correct workspace

callouts.text: 📖 Detailed guide: Microsoft Entra ID SSO Setup

substeps.title: 2a. Verify your domain in Claude
items: Go to [Claude Admin → Identity and access](https://claude.ai/admin-settings/identity)
Add your company domain and follow the verification steps
Requires adding a DNS TXT record (coordinate with your DNS admin)

substeps.title: 2b. Start SSO setup flow in Claude
items: In Identity and access settings, start the SSO configuration
Note the **Entity ID** and **Reply URL (ACS URL)** — share these with your Identity team
Keep this flow open while the Identity team configures Entra

substeps.title: 2c. Create Enterprise Application in Entra
items: In Entra Admin Center → Enterprise applications → New application
Search for "Claude" in gallery, or create your own app named "Claude"
Select "Integrate any other application you don’t find in the gallery"

substeps.title: 2d. Configure SAML settings
items: Go to Single sign-on → SAML
In Basic SAML Configuration, enter the Entity ID and Reply URL from step 2b
Set Sign-on URL: `https://claude.ai/login`
In Attributes & Claims, set email claim to `user.mail`
Download the **Federation Metadata XML**

substeps.title: 2e. Upload metadata & complete in Claude
items: Identity team shares the Federation Metadata XML with Primary Owner
Primary Owner uploads it in the Claude SSO setup flow
Complete the connection wizard in Claude

substeps.title: 2f. Configure SCIM provisioning (optional)
items: Requires Entra ID P1 or P2 license
In the app → Provisioning → Set mode to Automatic
Enter Tenant URL and Secret Token from Claude setup flow
Ensure SCIM email attribute matches SSO email claim (`user.mail`)
Set Provisioning Status to On

substeps.title: 2g. Assign users/groups & verify
items: In Users and groups, assign people or groups who should access Claude
Trigger a provisioning cycle (if SCIM enabled) and confirm users appear in Claude
Have a test user complete SSO login → verify they land in the correct workspace

steps.title: Step 3: Invite Users
steps.description: How users get access depends on whether SSO is configured yet.
items: **Pre-SSO:** Manually invite early users via the Admin Console (they'll use magic-link login)
**Post-SSO:** End users go to [claude.ai/login](https://claude.ai/login) → enter company email → get redirected through your corporate IdP automatically
**With SCIM:** Users are auto-provisioned when assigned in your IdP — no manual invites needed
**With JIT (Just-in-Time):** Users are created on first SSO login if they match the verified domain

callouts.text: ✅ **Done!** Your Claude Enterprise workspace is now configured. Users on your domain can access Claude at claude.ai/login using company credentials.

content.heading: Migration Scenarios
content.description: Common paths for migrating existing Claude usage to Claude for Enterprise on AWS.

options.title: Self-owned Enterprise → Enterprise on AWS Marketplace
items: Work with Anthropic to **reuse the same workspace UUID**
Users and data are retained as-is — no re-provisioning needed
Billing moves to AWS Marketplace; workspace stays intact
Contact your Anthropic account team to initiate the transfer

options.title: Domain Claim (Personal & Team Subscriptions)
items: Works for pre-existing **personal** and **team plan** users on your company email domain
Admin initiates domain claim — existing users are notified and absorbed into Enterprise workspace
All user data (conversations, projects) migrates automatically
Users see a notification and can accept the move or export their data

options.title: Enterprise-to-Enterprise Migration (Manual)
items: There is **no built-in tool** to migrate between one Enterprise org and another
Use the Project Migration Tool to move projects one-by-one
Interactive migration tool will be embedded below when ready

content.heading: Claude on Amazon Bedrock
content.description: Access Claude models as a fully managed API through Amazon Bedrock.

sections.label: Overview
sections.heading: Claude Code & Claude Cowork via Amazon Bedrock
sections.description: Deploy Claude Code (CLI/IDE AI coding assistant) and Claude Cowork (desktop AI companion) through Amazon Bedrock. All data stays within your AWS boundary — Anthropic never sees prompts or completions.

items.title: Claude Code
items: AI coding assistant (CLI/IDE-integrated) — code generation, review, debugging, refactoring
Uses AWS SDK credential chain to authenticate to Bedrock
For development teams

items.title: Claude Cowork
items: AI desktop companion app — delegates long-running tasks, works with local files, MCP connectors
In-app AWS Sign-in via IAM Identity Center (no CLI needed on end-user machines)
For all teams — document analysis, research, workflow automation
Per-team dedicated AWS accounts for cost isolation and independent governance
Centralized identity via IAM Identity Center federated with Microsoft Entra ID
Data stays within AWS boundary — Anthropic never sees prompts or completions
Team-specific model access and configuration profiles
CloudTrail audit logging with per-user attribution

sections.label: Prerequisites & Setup
sections.heading: Prerequisites & Account Setup
sections.description: Foundational setup required before rolling out Claude to any team.

steps.title: AWS Organizations Setup
items: AWS Organizations must be configured with a well-defined OU structure
**Option A:** Create a dedicated OU for Claude workload accounts (e.g., `OU: AI-Assistants`) — simplifies SCP inheritance
**Option B:** Place Claude accounts within existing team/BU OUs — aligns with current org structure but requires per-account SCP attachment
Each team gets a dedicated AWS account
Management account retains central governance and SCP attachment

steps.title: IAM Identity Center Configuration
items: **Enable IAM Identity Center** in the management account (or delegated admin)
**Configure Microsoft Entra ID** as external identity source — SAML 2.0 federation + SCIM provisioning
**Create Entra ID Security Groups** per team (e.g., `SG-Claude-TeamAlpha`) for SCIM sync
**Set session duration** to 8-12 hours for permission sets (controls re-auth frequency)

steps.title: Service Control Policies (SCPs)
steps.description: Apply these SCPs to the Claude OU to enforce guardrails across all team accounts:
items: Deny `bedrock:InvokeModel` unless InferenceProfileArn matches `us.anthropic.claude-*`
Prevents teams from invoking non-Claude or unapproved models
Deny `bedrock:PutUseCaseForModelAccess` except for `CentralAdminRole`
Prevents teams from enabling additional models without central approval
Deny `bedrock:*` unless `aws:RequestedRegion` is in approved list (e.g., us-east-1, us-west-2)
Adjust regions based on data residency and latency requirements

substeps.title: Restrict Bedrock to Approved Models Only
items: Deny `bedrock:InvokeModel` unless InferenceProfileArn matches `us.anthropic.claude-*`
Prevents teams from invoking non-Claude or unapproved models

substeps.title: Deny Model Access Changes
items: Deny `bedrock:PutUseCaseForModelAccess` except for `CentralAdminRole`
Prevents teams from enabling additional models without central approval

substeps.title: Enforce Region Restriction
items: Deny `bedrock:*` unless `aws:RequestedRegion` is in approved list (e.g., us-east-1, us-west-2)
Adjust regions based on data residency and latency requirements

steps.title: Bedrock Service Limits & Quotas
steps.description: For each new team account, configure:
items: Request appropriate model invocation quotas (TPM) based on team size
Set up AWS Budgets with alerts at 80% and 100% thresholds
Configure CloudWatch alarms for throttling (`InvocationThrottles` metric)
Enable Bedrock Invocation Logging to S3 for audit trail

steps.title: Shared Account vs. Dedicated Account
steps.description: If a team plans to use an existing AWS account with Bedrock workloads:
items: Review current quota utilization (CloudWatch: InvocationCount, TokenCount, InvocationThrottles)
Assess whether remaining headroom can absorb expected Claude traffic
If quota headroom insufficient or workloads are mission-critical → **create a separate dedicated account**
If sharing is acceptable → request quota increases + set up Application Inference Profiles for cost separation

callouts.text: ⚠️ **Key Risks of Sharing:** Quota contention, cost attribution complexity, budget alert noise, broader security blast radius.
💡 **Best practice:** Provision a dedicated AWS account per team. Eliminates quota contention, simplifies cost tracking, and ensures precise SCP scoping.

steps.title: Anthropic Use Case Submission
steps.description: One-time per account — required before Claude models can be invoked via Bedrock.
items: Navigate to Bedrock console > Model catalog
Select any Anthropic model and complete the use case form
Access granted immediately after submission
Create form.json with company details and use case description
Run the CLI command below (must target us-east-1)
Submitting principal needs `bedrock:PutUseCaseForModelAccess` IAM permission

callouts.text: 📋 **Why is this needed?** Anthropic requires every AWS account to submit a brief use-case form before enabling access to Claude models on Bedrock. This is a compliance and responsible-use requirement from Anthropic — it helps them understand how their models are being used and ensures alignment with their Acceptable Use Policy. The form is lightweight (company name, website, intended use case) and access is typically granted immediately upon submission.

substeps.title: Option A: Via AWS Console
items: Navigate to Bedrock console > Model catalog
Select any Anthropic model and complete the use case form
Access granted immediately after submission

substeps.title: Option B: Via CLI (recommended for automation)
items: Create form.json with company details and use case description
Run the CLI command below (must target us-east-1)
Submitting principal needs `bedrock:PutUseCaseForModelAccess` IAM permission

code.content:
# Create the form data JSON
cat  form.json
{
  "companyName": "YOUR_COMPANY",
  "companyWebsite": "https://www.example.com",
  "intendedUsers": "0",
  "industryOption": "Customer Service",
  "otherIndustryOption": "",
  "useCases": "AI-powered productivity tools."
}
EOF

# Submit (must be us-east-1, must Base64 encode)
aws bedrock put-use-case-for-model-access \
  --form-data $(cat form.json | base64) \
  --region us-east-1

code_blocks.content:
# Create the form data JSON
cat  form.json
{
  "companyName": "YOUR_COMPANY",
  "companyWebsite": "https://www.example.com",
  "intendedUsers": "0",
  "industryOption": "Customer Service",
  "otherIndustryOption": "",
  "useCases": "AI-powered productivity tools."
}
EOF

# Submit (must be us-east-1, must Base64 encode)
aws bedrock put-use-case-for-model-access \
  --form-data $(cat form.json | base64) \
  --region us-east-1

rows: Verify SCPs inherited from Claude OU
Create IAM Identity Center permission set
Assign Entra ID group to permission set + account
Set up AWS Budgets and cost alerts
Request and validate Bedrock quota limits
Test end-to-end auth flow with pilot user

sections.label: Claude Code Deployment
sections.heading: Claude Code Deployment
sections.description: CLI-based AI coding assistant that integrates into terminal and IDE workflows.

substeps.title: 3.1 IAM Permission Set
items: Create permission set in IAM Identity Center with inline policy
Grant `bedrock:InvokeModel` + `InvokeModelWithResponseStream`
Scope to specific model ARNs: Opus 4.7, Sonnet 4.6, Haiku 4.5
Set session duration to 8-12 hours
Each model is a separate statement — easy to add/remove per team

substeps.title: 3.2 Developer Machine Setup
items: **Prerequisites:** AWS CLI v2, Claude Code CLI (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`), network access to Bedrock + Identity Center
**AWS SSO Profile:** Configure `~/.aws/config` with SSO session, account ID, role name
**Environment Variables:** `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1`, `AWS_REGION`, `AWS_PROFILE`
**Model Pinning:** Set `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, etc.
Cross-region inference profile IDs (`us.` prefix) recommended for higher availability

substeps.title: 3.3 Managed Settings
items: **macOS:** `/Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/managed-settings.json`
**Linux/WSL:** `/etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json`
**Windows:** `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode\managed-settings.json`
Controls: permissions (allow/deny), MCP server restrictions, sandbox networking, environment variables
Set `allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly: true` and `allowManagedMcpServersOnly: true`

substeps.title: 3.4 Verification
items: `aws sso login --profile claude-code`
`claude` — launches Claude Code
`/status` — should show "Amazon Bedrock" as provider and list pinned models

substeps.title: 3.5 VS Code Extension
items: Shares same AWS credential chain as Claude Code CLI
If CLI works with SSO profile, extension picks it up automatically
Run `claude configure` → select Amazon Bedrock → specify SSO profile
Settings stored in `~/.claude/settings.json` — extension reads same config
Restart extension if VS Code was open during SSO login

sections.label: Claude Cowork Deployment
sections.heading: Claude Cowork Deployment
sections.description: Desktop AI companion app — all prompts route through your AWS infrastructure.

substeps.title: 4.1 Authentication: In-App AWS Sign-In
items: User launches Cowork → "Sign in with AWS" page
Opens browser for IAM Identity Center device-authorization flow
Authenticates via Entra ID (corporate credentials + MFA)
Token stored encrypted in OS secure storage
Re-auth when session expires (8-12 hours)

substeps.title: 4.2 Manual Configuration (Initial Rollout)
items: Install Claude Desktop from claude.com/download — **Do NOT sign in**
Enable Developer Mode: Help > Troubleshooting > Enable Developer Mode
Open: Developer > Configure third-party inference
Set: Inference Provider = Bedrock, Region, SSO Start URL, SSO Region, Account ID, Role Name
Configure Model List with Bedrock inference profile IDs
Click "Apply Locally" → app relaunches with "Sign in with AWS" option
⚠️ Remove `inferenceBedrockProfile` field to enable in-app AWS sign-in

substeps.title: 4.3 MDM-Based Configuration (Future State)
items: Build config on admin machine → Export .mobileconfig (macOS) or .reg (Windows)
Deploy config via MDM (Jamf, Intune, Workspace ONE) BEFORE distributing app
**macOS:** `/Library/Managed Preferences/com.anthropic.claudefordesktop.plist`
**Windows:** `HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Claude`
Key benefit: in-app configuration window becomes read-only on managed devices
For Intune MSIX deployment: Apps > Windows > Line-of-business > Upload .msix
For ADMX policy: Import Claude template, create configuration policy per team

sections.label: SOPs
sections.heading: Standard Operating Procedures
sections.description: Repeatable processes for common administrative actions.

substeps.title: SOP-1: Onboard New User to Existing Team
items: Add user to Entra ID security group → SCIM auto-syncs within 40 min
Deploy Claude tools to user machine (CLI + config or Cowork + managed config)
User acceptance test → verify end-to-end flow
Send onboarding email with usage guidelines

substeps.title: SOP-2: Onboard New Team
items: Create dedicated AWS account under Claude OU
Submit Anthropic use case form
Create Entra ID group + configure SCIM
Create permission set + assign group to account
Configure budgets, monitoring, quotas
Pilot with 3-5 users → full rollout

substeps.title: SOP-3: Modify Model Access
items: Evaluate cost impact (Opus ≈ 5x Sonnet per token), quota needs, region availability
Update SCP allowed model list if needed
Update managed-settings.json and Cowork MDM profiles
Adjust budget alerts for new cost profile

substeps.title: SOP-4: Revoke User Access
items: Remove user from Entra ID security group (SCIM auto-deprovisions)
For immediate revocation: delete user session in IAM Identity Center
Remove CLI + managed-settings and Cowork app from device
Verify via CloudTrail — no post-revocation API calls

substeps.title: SOP-5: Decommission a Team
items: Remove all users from Entra group
Delete permission set assignment
Disable Bedrock model access
Archive CloudTrail + invocation logs
Close/suspend AWS account (retain for audit period)
Remove MDM profiles + delete Entra group

sections.label: Monitoring & Cost
sections.heading: Monitoring & Cost Management
sections.description: Track usage, attribute costs, and enforce quotas.

substeps.title: Cost Attribution (Account + Per-User)
items: **Account-level:** Per-team accounts → Cost Explorer shows each team’s spend
**Per-user:** Enable "IAM principal-based cost allocation" in Cost Explorer
Each Identity Center user gets unique role session → per-user spend visibility
⚠️ Principal-based allocation is **visibility only**, not enforcement

substeps.title: Quota Enforcement (OTel + Counter Store)
items: Claude Code & Cowork export OTel metrics natively (token usage, cost, sessions per user) Custom implementation needed
Data flow: Client → OTLP (port 4318) → OTel Collector (ECS Fargate) → CloudWatch/S3
Counter store (Redis): aggregate per-user token usage with TTL for auto-reset
**Alert only:** SNS notification when threshold exceeded (15 min lag)
**Soft block:** Lambda updates IAM deny policy (15-30 min)
**Hard block:** Proxy checks Redis pre-request (real-time)

substeps.title: Recommended CloudWatch Alarms
items: `InvocationThrottles > 10` in 5 minutes (quota exhaustion)
`InvocationErrors > 5%` error rate (configuration issues)
Monthly cost exceeds 80% of budget threshold

sections.label: Security & Compliance
sections.heading: Security & Compliance
sections.description: Data handling, guardrails, network security, and audit controls.

substeps.title: Data Handling
items: All prompts/completions flow through AWS Bedrock — Anthropic does not store or access them
Data stays within AWS security boundary (your VPC/account)
Anthropic does NOT train models on your data via Bedrock
Zero Data Retention (ZDR) available — nothing stored after request completes

substeps.title: Bedrock Guardrails
items: Create Guardrails in Bedrock console: content filtering, PII redaction, topic blocking
Safeguards: content filters, denied topics, word filters, sensitive information filters, contextual grounding
⚠️ Do NOT enable automated reasoning policy — unsupported, causes runtime failures
**Cross-account enforcement:** AWS Organizations Bedrock policies apply guardrails across all team accounts
Create guardrail in management account → version it → attach resource-based policy → enable Bedrock Policy Type → attach to Claude OU

substeps.title: Network Security
items: Consider AWS PrivateLink for Bedrock endpoints (keeps traffic off public internet)
Use sandbox network allowlists in Claude Code/Cowork
Ensure corporate firewall allows required AWS endpoint traffic

substeps.title: Audit & Compliance
items: CloudTrail: complete audit trail with user identity
IAM Identity Center sessions traceable to Entra ID users
Bedrock invocation logs capture full request/response
Managed settings prevent bypassing organizational controls

content.heading: CCWB (Claude Code WorkBench)
content.description: Deploy Claude Code and Claude Cowork on Amazon Bedrock using IAM Identity Center.

sections.label: Prerequisites & Environment Setup
sections.heading: Prerequisites & Environment Setup
sections.description: What you need before deploying CCWB with IAM Identity Center.

subsections.title: Software Requirements (Admin Machine)
items: Python 3.10–3.13
Poetry (dependency management)
AWS CLI v2
Git
Go 1.23+ (for cross-platform binary builds)

subsections.title: AWS Requirements
items: AWS account with IAM/CloudFormation permissions
Amazon Bedrock activated in target regions
IAM Identity Center active with users/groups provisioned
(Optional) ECS, CloudWatch, Athena, Glue, Lambda, Firehose permissions — for monitoring/analytics

subsections.title: End User Requirements
items: Claude Code installed (for CLI users)
Claude Desktop installed (for CoWork users)
Web browser for SSO authentication
No Python/Poetry/Git needed — users get pre-built packages

sections.label: Configure IAM Identity Center
sections.heading: Configure IAM Identity Center
sections.description: Set up the native AWS identity path — no external IdP required.
items: **Verify IAM Identity Center is enabled** — Navigate to AWS IAM Identity Center console and confirm it's active in your management account
**Provision users/groups** — Ensure developers who need Claude access exist in IAM Identity Center (synced from AD/Okta/external source or manually created)
**Create Permission Set for Bedrock access** — Create a Permission Set with `bedrock:InvokeModel*` permissions scoped to target regions
**Assign Permission Set to user/group** — Assign the Permission Set to developer groups on the target AWS account
**Set session duration** — Recommended: 7 days (max: 90 days) to minimize re-authentication friction
**Distribute SSO start URL** — Share the SSO portal URL and profile name with developers (e.g., `aws sso login --profile bedrock-dev`)

callouts.text: **💡 Key benefit:** With IAM Identity Center, the CCWB solution auto-detects user identity from the `AWSReservedSSO_*` role ARN — full attribution (name, email, permission set) appears in the observability dashboard without any extra OIDC configuration.

sections.label: Initialize Profile (`ccwb init`)
sections.heading: Initialize Profile (`ccwb init`)
sections.description: Clone the repo and run the interactive setup wizard to create a deployment profile.

subsections.title: Wizard Choices (IAM IDC Path)
items: **Profile name** → e.g. `corp-bedrock-prod`
**Enable SSO authentication?** → Select **No** (this refers to CCWB's own OIDC auth stack — select No because your users already authenticate via IAM Identity Center)
**AWS Region** → Region for CloudFormation stacks
**Stack base name** → Prefix for all stack names (e.g. `claude-code-auth`)
**Enable monitoring?** → Yes (recommended) — choose Central Collector or Sidecar
**Enable analytics?** → Yes for Athena + S3 data lake
**Enable quota monitoring?** → Yes to enforce per-user token limits
**Enable Windows builds?** → Yes if you have Windows users
**Generate CoWork 3P MDM config?** → Yes for Claude Desktop deployment
**Claude model** → Sonnet / Haiku / Opus
**Cross-region profile** → US / EU / APAC
**Source region** → e.g. `us-east-1`

callouts.text: **What is `ccwb`?** — CCWB (Claude Code WorkBench) is the CLI tool from the [AWS Solutions guidance repo](https://github.com/aws-solutions-library-samples/guidance-for-claude-code-with-amazon-bedrock). It orchestrates the full deployment lifecycle: `init` creates a local profile, `deploy` provisions CloudFormation stacks, `package` builds platform-specific installers, and `distribute` shares them with end users.
**📝 Note:** `ccwb init` only saves configuration locally to `~/.ccwb/profiles/<name>.json`. Nothing is deployed to AWS at this stage.

sections.label: Deploy Infrastructure (`ccwb deploy`)
sections.heading: Deploy Infrastructure (`ccwb deploy`)
sections.description: Provision AWS CloudFormation stacks based on your profile configuration.

subsections.title: What Gets Created (IAM IDC path)
items: **Monitoring stack** — VPC, ECS Fargate (OTEL collector), ALB, CloudWatch dashboards
**Analytics stack** — Kinesis Firehose → S3 → Glue → Athena (if enabled)
**Quota stack** — DynamoDB (limits), Lambda (monitor), SNS (alerts), API Gateway (if enabled)
**CodeBuild stack** — Windows .exe compilation (if enabled)

callouts.text: **What `ccwb deploy` does under the hood:** Reads your local profile and creates CloudFormation stacks in your AWS account. Since users already authenticate via IAM Identity Center (`aws sso login`), there's no need for CCWB to deploy its own auth stack (OIDC Provider / Cognito). It deploys only the monitoring, analytics, and quota stacks you enabled.
**⏱️ Deployment time:** 5–15 minutes depending on which stacks are enabled.

sections.label: Build & Distribute Packages (`ccwb package`)
sections.heading: Build & Distribute Packages (`ccwb package`)
sections.description: Build platform-specific installers and distribute to end users.

subsections.title: Platform Targets
items: macOS ARM64 (Apple Silicon M1/M2/M3/M4)
macOS Intel (x86_64) — runs on all Macs via Rosetta
Linux x86_64 and ARM64
Windows x64 (via CodeBuild + Nuitka)

subsections.title: Distribution Options
items: **Presigned S3 URLs** — Time-limited download links shared via Slack/email
**Authenticated Landing Page** — Self-service portal with SSO login + platform detection
**Manual** — Zip the `dist/` folder and share directly

subsections.title: Generate CoWork MDM Configs
subsections.description:
poetry run ccwb cowork generate⧉ Copy
          Outputs: cowork-3p.mobileconfig, cowork-3p.reg, cowork-3p-config.json

callouts.text: **What `ccwb package` does:** Compiles the credential-helper binary for each target platform using Go cross-compilation. The binary handles token refresh, OTEL metric reporting, and AWS credential resolution. The resulting installers configure the user's `~/.aws/config` with a `credential_process` entry pointing to this binary.

sections.label: MDM Deployment (Claude Cowork / Desktop)
sections.heading: MDM Deployment (Claude Cowork / Desktop)
sections.description: Deploy Claude Desktop configuration to end-user machines via MDM.
items: **macOS** — Deploy `cowork-3p.mobileconfig` via Jamf / Kandji / Mosyle
**Windows** — Deploy `cowork-3p.reg` via Intune / Group Policy (writes to `HKCU\SOFTWARE\Policies\Claude`)
**Verify MDM key** — Ensure `inferenceBedrockProfile` points to the correct AWS named profile in `~/.aws/config`
**Install order matters** — Users must run the CLI installer (`install.sh` / `install.bat`) *before* opening Claude Desktop, so the named profile exists

callouts.text: **⚠️ macOS note:** Unsigned profiles require user approval in System Settings → Privacy & Security → Profiles after delivery.

sections.label: Developer Setup
sections.heading: Developer Setup
sections.description: End-user steps to get Claude Code / Claude Cowork working.

subsections.title: Claude Code (CLI)
items: Run the installer package provided by IT admin (`install.sh` or `install.bat`)
Authenticate with IAM Identity Center: `aws sso login --profile <profile-name>`
Start using Claude Code — credentials auto-refresh from IAM Identity Center session

subsections.title: Claude Cowork (Desktop)
items: Ensure installer has been run (sets up AWS named profile)
Open Claude Desktop — authentication is handled automatically by the credential helper
Full Claude Desktop capabilities: Projects, Artifacts, MCP servers

callouts.text: **💡 No API keys needed:** Developers authenticate with their existing corporate credentials via `aws sso login`. Temporary credentials are issued and refreshed automatically.

sections.label: Observability
sections.heading: Observability
sections.description: End-to-end visibility into Claude usage across your organization.

subsections.title: Architecture
items: **OpenTelemetry (OTLP)** — Credential helper emits metrics on every Bedrock call (tokens, latency, model, user identity)
**Central Collector (ECS Fargate)** — Receives OTLP from all developer machines, exports to CloudWatch EMF + Firehose
**Sidecar Collector (local)** — Runs on each dev machine, lighter footprint, no VPC needed

subsections.title: Identity Attribution (IAM IDC)
items: IAM Identity Center users: real username/email auto-extracted from `AWSReservedSSO_*` role ARN
IAM users: username extracted from IAM user ARN
Non-SSO assumed roles: hashed anonymous identifier (consistent per principal)

subsections.title: Analytics Pipeline (Optional)
items: Kinesis Data Firehose → S3 data lake → Glue Data Catalog → Athena
10 pre-built SQL queries for historical analysis
90-day hot storage + Glacier archival
Use for: chargeback, cost attribution by team/department, trend analysis

sections.label: Monitoring
sections.heading: Monitoring
sections.description: CloudWatch dashboards and real-time metrics for Claude usage.

subsections.title: Dashboard Metrics (PromQL)
items: **Token usage** — Input/output tokens per user, per model, per day
**Cost tracking** — Estimated cost per user/team based on token consumption
**Model breakdown** — Usage split across Sonnet / Haiku / Opus
**Latency** — P50/P95/P99 response times per model
**Error rates** — Throttling, failures, quota exhaustion

subsections.title: Infrastructure
items: **Central Collector:** ~$30–50/month (VPC + ECS Fargate + ALB)
**Sidecar:** $0 server cost (runs locally on each dev machine)
HTTPS with custom domain (optional) — ACM cert + Route53

code.content:
# Re-run init to enable monitoring later
poetry run ccwb init

# Deploy just the monitoring stack
poetry run ccwb deploy monitoring

sections.label: Quota Management
sections.heading: Quota Management
sections.description: Enforce per-user and per-group token limits with alerts and blocking.

subsections.title: How It Works
items: **DynamoDB table** — Stores per-user/group/default token limits
**Lambda (every 15 min)** — Checks thresholds via PromQL, sends SNS alerts at 80%, 90%, 100%
**API Gateway** — Real-time quota check at credential issuance time

subsections.title: Configuration Options
items: **Monthly token limit** — Default: 225M tokens/user/month
**Burst buffer** — 5–25% above daily average (default 10%)
**Daily enforcement** — `alert` (notify only) or `block` (deny credentials)
**Monthly enforcement** — `alert` or `block` (recommended: block)
**Check interval** — 0 min (every request, +200ms) / 30 min (default) / 60 min

callouts.text: **⚠️ Note:** Per-user quota enforcement requires the monitoring stack to be enabled. Without monitoring, only aggregate limits are possible.

content.heading: Claude Platform on AWS
content.description: Deploy the Claude Platform on your AWS infrastructure. Full control over networking, data residency, and scaling — ideal for regulated environments requiring VPC isolation.
